questions: Can I proofread my own work?
answers: Proofreading is the final review of a document to catch surface-level errors like typos, spelling mistakes, punctuation issues, and formatting inconsistencies.
Proofreading holo document er final check — typo, spelling mistake, punctuation error, ar formatting issue dhorar jonno.
To proofread effectively, read slowly, read aloud, change the font or print it out, check one type of error at a time, and take breaks between writing and proofing.
You can proofread your own work, but it's harder because your brain auto-corrects familiar text. Reading backward or using text-to-speech tools can help catch more errors.
